response: The global food system faces mounting strain as the population is projected to reach 9.7 billion by 2050, requiring about 60% more food production. This challenge is intensified by climate change, water scarcity, soil degradation, and food waste: agriculture produces roughly 25% of greenhouse gas emissions, 33% of the world’s arable land has lost productivity, and about one-third of all food is wasted. The text argues that feeding the future sustainably will require a combination of technological, agricultural, and policy solutions, including AI-driven precision farming, vertical farming, alternative proteins, drought-resistant crops, regenerative agriculture, and reforms to subsidies and carbon pricing. The central challenge is scaling these solutions while keeping food affordable and accessible, especially for the poorest populations.
